symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden well-demarcated round patches of hair loss

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Discrete round non-scarring patches are characteristic of AA.
Telogen effluvium is diffuse, not focal patches.

symptoms.symptom_name: Smooth normal scalp — no scale, pustules, crusting or erythema

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of scale/pustules/crusts/nodes argues strongly against inflammatory tinea.
No erythema/plugging/loss of ostia argues against scarring alopecia.
Exclamation-mark hairs are highly specific for active alopecia areata.
AA associates with other autoimmune disease such as autoimmune thyroiditis.
Acute health-catastrophising commonly triggers panic; requires safety screening.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: AA is a clinical/dermoscopic diagnosis; no urgent bloods needed acutely.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Thyroid function / autoimmune screen via GP
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Given associated autoimmunity, review thyroid status and consider screening as an outpatient.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Fungal scalp scraping if any scale/pustules appear
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Only if morphology changes to suggest tinea.
